place: Cape May Court House, New Jersey

facts.text: Cape May Court House was laid out in 1703 by Jeremiah Hand and was first called Rumney Marsh and Middleton before adopting its present name.
The county seat’s courthouse was first built on Daniel Hand’s property in 1764, replacing earlier meetings in homes and a church.
The current courthouse structure dates to 1849, replacing the original building for the county.
The arrival of the railroad in the 1880s turned the town into a regional hub for produce and goods.
The Cape May County Museum, housed in the courthouse, preserves and interprets the county’s history and cultural heritage.
Cape May Court House is the location of the Cape May County Library’s branch, serving the community’s reading needs.
